{"step_by_step_thinking": "The question involves comparing the rates of extubation failure between two independent groups (conventional CPAP and bubble CPAP). The data provided are counts of successes and failures in each group, which suggests a categorical variable (success/failure) between two independent groups. Given these characteristics, the Chi-square test is the most appropriate statistical test to compare the proportions of extubation failure between the two methods. The paired test, Student test, and ANOVA are not suitable for this scenario as they do not appropriately handle the comparison of proportions between independent groups.", "answer_choice": "B"}
